Skip to contents
centrcanon 0.1.0
  • Reference
    • The CEnTR*MAP Analytical Pipeline

CLAUDE.md — centrcanon

Source: CLAUDE.md

Guidance for Claude Code when generating, editing, or extending this R package.

What this package is

centrcanon is an open-source R package that provides the analytical engine for CEnTR*MAP — a Shiny-based workflow supporting institutions and communities in collaboratively constructing a local definition of community-engaged research (CEnR).

The end product is a definition, not just a map

The package does not produce a definition directly. It produces scores that help practitioners identify which concepts are most central, structurally connected, and influential in their institution’s conceptual landscape. Those scores are then used — by faculty, community partners, or working groups — to organize, prioritize, and synthesize terms into a locally-grounded, community-constructed definition of CEnR.

In other words: the map is a means, not the end. The three pipelines surface the underlying structure of how a community of scholars talks about their work, so that structure can inform the language they ultimately adopt.

The three pipelines

Pipeline Question answered Output score
Anchoring Which concepts are most central to how this community talks about CEnR? anchoring_score
Integration Which concepts serve as structural connectors across the network? integration_score
Leverage Which concepts hold the most influence in shaping the field? leverage_score

Together, high scores across all three pipelines identify the strongest candidates for inclusion in a local CEnR definition. Clusters (from PAM) and communities (from network detection) suggest how terms can be grouped into coherent themes or commitments within that definition.

The Shiny application that calls this package is closed-source and paywalled. The package itself must remain completely standalone — no Shiny imports, no UI code, no app-specific configuration. All functions must work from the R console.

Style guide

Follow the Tidyverse Style Guide throughout. Key rules:

  • snake_case for all function names, argument names, and variables.
  • Informative verb-noun names: calculate_anchoring_score(), create_network(), classify_node_role(). Avoid generic names like process() or run().
  • One function per file is a good default; group tightly related small helpers together (e.g., utils-rank.R).
  • No lines over 80 characters. Wrap with the operator or pipe at the end of the preceding line.
  • Use the native pipe |> (R ≥ 4.1), not %>%, unless a package-specific pipe is unavoidable.
  • No library() or require() calls inside package functions. Use package::function() for non-imported calls, or declare in NAMESPACE via @importFrom.
  • @param, @return, @examples, and @export roxygen tags are required for every exported function.
  • Internal helpers get @noRd and no @export.
  • Prefer tibble output over bare data.frame for all user-facing functions.
  • Use dplyr/tidyr idioms for data manipulation; avoid base R apply loops unless performance demands it.
  • Numeric output columns should be double; categorical outputs should be factor with explicit levels.

Package structure

centrcanon/
├── DESCRIPTION
├── NAMESPACE                  # generated by roxygen2, never edit by hand
├── R/
│   ├── tier.R                 # calculate_tier()
│   ├── ca_analysis.R          # prepare_contingency_table(), run_correspondence_analysis()
│   ├── pam_clustering.R       # run_pam_clustering()
│   ├── anchoring.R            # calculate_anchoring_score()
│   ├── network.R              # create_network(), calculate_network_metrics()
│   ├── integration.R          # calculate_integration_score()
│   ├── leverage.R             # classify_node_role(), calculate_leverage_score()
│   ├── palettes.R             # earthtone_palette(), centr_palette()  [unexported constants]
│   └── utils-rank.R           # dense_rank_normalize(), minmax_scale(), calculate_entropy()
├── tests/
│   └── testthat/
│       ├── test-tier.R
│       ├── test-anchoring.R
│       ├── test-integration.R
│       └── test-leverage.R
├── vignettes/
│   └── centr-pipeline.Rmd     # end-to-end walkthrough
├── man/                       # generated by roxygen2
├── data-raw/                  # scripts that produce any bundled test data
└── inst/
    └── extdata/               # small example CSV for vignette / tests

Analytical pipelines — function contracts

Shared utilities (utils-rank.R)

# Min-max normalize a numeric vector; returns 0.5 for constant input
minmax_scale(x)  -> double vector in [0, 1]

# Shannon entropy normalized by log(n), where n = length(values)
calculate_entropy(values)  -> double scalar in [0, 1]

# Dense-rank then normalize to [0, 1]
dense_rank_normalize(x)  -> double vector in [0, 1]

Tier classification (tier.R)

calculate_tier(scores)
  • Cuts scores at the 40th, 60th, and 80th percentiles.
  • Returns an ordered factor with levels "Low" < "Medium" < "High" < "Very High".
  • Must handle NA values via na.rm = TRUE on internal quantile() calls.

Pipeline 1 — Anchoring (ca_analysis.R, pam_clustering.R, anchoring.R)

Step 0 — Prepare contingency table

prepare_contingency_table(edgelist)  -> matrix (groups × descriptors)
  • Input: a tibble with columns group, from, to (the caller has already renamed raw source columns, e.g. hex1/hex2, to from/to).
  • Pivots from and to into a single descriptor column so that both endpoints of each edge are treated as concept descriptors co-occurring with their group.
  • Filters out NA and empty-string descriptors.
  • Cross-tabulates group × descriptor via table(), then removes zero-sum rows and columns.
  • Returns a matrix suitable for passing directly to run_correspondence_analysis().

Step 1 — Correspondence analysis

run_correspondence_analysis(contingency_table)  -> FactoMineR::CA object

Step 2 — PAM clustering on CA coordinates

run_pam_clustering(ca_result, k = 6L)  -> cluster::pam object
  • Default k = 6L; caller may override.
  • Clusters on dimensions 1–2 of ca_result$col$coord.

Step 3 — Anchoring score

calculate_anchoring_score(ca_result, cluster_assignments)

Returns a tibble with columns:

Column Type Description
concept character concept / descriptor name
cluster integer PAM cluster id
salience_raw double CA column inertia
dimensional_raw double sum of contributions to dims 1 & 2
cluster_exemplar_raw double proximity-weighted cos2 score
rank_salience integer dense rank of salience_raw
rank_dimensional integer dense rank of dimensional_raw
rank_exemplar integer dense rank of cluster_exemplar_raw
combined_rank double mean of the three ranks
anchoring_score double normalized to [0, 1]
percentile_rank double 0–100
anchoring_category ordered factor “Peripheral” < “Supporting” < “Secondary” < “Primary” anchor
tier ordered factor from calculate_tier()

The exemplar sub-score logic: - For each cluster, compute the cluster centroid as apply(coords, 2, median). - Distance from centroid = Euclidean; invert and weight by cos2_total (sum of cos2 across dims 1 & 2). - Single-member clusters get cos2_total directly as their exemplar score.

All three sub-scores are equally weighted (no boosting parameters in the default exported function; leave a clearly documented argument stub if needed for future extension).

Pipeline 2 — Integration (network.R, integration.R)

Network construction

create_network(edgelist)  -> igraph::igraph object
  • Input: a two-column data frame / tibble with columns from and to (lowercase; document the rename from the source From/To).
  • Calls igraph::simplify() to remove loops and multi-edges.
  • Returns an undirected graph.

Network metrics

calculate_network_metrics(graph)

Returns a tibble with one row per node and columns:

Column Source
name V(g)$name
degree igraph::degree(g, normalized = TRUE)
betweenness igraph::betweenness(g, normalized = TRUE)
crossclique centiserve::crossclique(g)
cflow centiserve::closeness.currentflow(g)
cbet centiserve::communibet(g, normalized = TRUE)
eigen igraph::eigen_centrality(g)$vector
katz centiserve::katzcent(g)
community igraph::cluster_optimal(g)$membership as factor
  • centiserve functions should be wrapped in tryCatch; on failure, fill the column with NA_real_ and emit a rlang::warn().
  • For graphs with vcount(g) <= 1, skip community detection and set community = factor(1).

Integration score

calculate_integration_score(network_metrics_tbl)

Returns the input tibble augmented with:

Column Logic
betweenness_scaled min-max of betweenness
cflow_scaled min-max of cflow
crossclique_scaled min-max of crossclique
rank_betweenness dense rank
rank_cflow dense rank
rank_crossclique dense rank
rank_sum sum of three ranks
integration_score normalized to [0, 1]
tier calculate_tier(integration_score)

Pipeline 3 — Leverage (leverage.R)

Leverage score + node classification

calculate_leverage_score(network_metrics_tbl)

Uses eigen, katz, and cbet columns (from calculate_network_metrics()). Returns input tibble augmented with:

Column Logic
eigen_z minmax_scale(eigen)
katz_z minmax_scale(katz)
cbet_z minmax_scale(cbet)
rank_eigen dense rank of eigen_z
rank_katz dense rank of katz_z
rank_cbet dense rank of cbet_z
rank_sum sum of three ranks
leverage_score normalized to [0, 1]
tier calculate_tier(leverage_score)
node_role factor from classify_node_role()

Node role classification

classify_node_role(eigen_z, cbet_z, katz_z)  -> character scalar

Internal helper (@noRd). Classification logic (evaluated in order):

  1. All three ≥ q90 → "Core Keystone"
  2. All three ≥ q75 and entropy ≥ 0.9 → "Supporting Keystone"
  3. All three ≥ q75 and entropy < 0.9 → paired type:
    • eigen + katz high → "Beacon"
    • eigen + cbet high → "Steward"
    • cbet + katz high → "Aqueduct"
    • else → "Hybrid"
  4. All three ≤ q25 → "Non-Key"
  5. Exactly one ≥ q75 → "Sage" (eigen), "Weaver" (cbet), "Messenger" (katz)
  6. Two ≥ q75 → paired logic as in (3)
  7. else → "Leaning"

Quantile thresholds (q25, q75, q90) must be computed over all nodes in the dataset being classified, not hardcoded. Pass them as arguments to the internal helper so tests can inject known values.

Node role levels (for the factor): "Core Keystone", "Supporting Keystone", "Beacon", "Steward", "Aqueduct", "Hybrid", "Sage", "Weaver", "Messenger", "Leaning", "Non-Key"

Color palettes (palettes.R)

Export two named character vectors — do not couple them to ggplot2 theme code (that belongs in the Shiny app):

# 8-color earthtone palette for cluster/role coloring
centr_earthtone_palette()  -> named character vector (hex)

# 3-color base palette for the three analytical layers
centr_layer_palette()  -> named character vector
# names: "anchoring", "integration", "leverage"

Gradient helpers for the Shiny app live in the app, not the package.

DESCRIPTION essentials

Package: centrcanon
Title: Analytical Engine for CEnTR*MAP Community-Engaged Research Mapping
Version: 0.1.0
License: MIT + file LICENSE
Encoding: UTF-8
Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE)
RoxygenNote: 7.x.x

Imports (hard dependencies — list in DESCRIPTION Imports:): dplyr, tidyr, tibble, rlang, FactoMineR, cluster, igraph

Suggests (soft dependencies — list in DESCRIPTION Suggests:): centiserve, expm, testthat (>= 3.0.0), knitr, rmarkdown, ggplot2, ggrepel, ggraph

centiserve is in Suggests, not Imports, because it is unavailable on CRAN. All calls to centiserve functions must be guarded:

if (requireNamespace("centiserve", quietly = TRUE)) {
  centiserve::crossclique(g)
} else {
  rlang::warn(
    "centiserve is not installed; `crossclique` set to NA. \\
     Install from GitHub: muuankarski/centiserve"
  )
  rep(NA_real_, igraph::vcount(g))
}

Testing

Use testthat (3rd edition). Declare Config/testthat/edition: 3 in DESCRIPTION.

Each test file mirrors its source file. Minimum coverage expectations:

  • test-tier.R: boundary values, NA handling, correct factor levels and ordering.
  • test-anchoring.R: prepare_contingency_table() produces correct dimensions and drops zero-sum rows/cols; score is in [0, 1]; single-concept cluster handled; factor levels correct; calculate_tier() output attached.
  • test-integration.R: score is in [0, 1]; disconnected graph returns valid result; centiserve-absent path returns NA columns with a warning.
  • test-leverage.R: each classify_node_role() branch reachable with a synthetic 3-node case; leverage_score in [0, 1].

Build minimal synthetic fixtures in tests/testthat/helper-fixtures.R rather than loading external CSV files.

Data input conventions

The package does not own raw data loading — that belongs to the calling app. The source CSV has columns group, hex1, hex2; the caller renames hex1/hex2 to from/to before passing the edgelist into any package function.

The single prepared input the package works from is:

Input Expected form
edgelist Tibble with columns group, from, to (lowercase). An optional weight column is ignored by the CA pipeline but used by the network pipeline if present.

From this edgelist, two analytical inputs are derived: - Contingency table — via prepare_contingency_table(edgelist), which the package provides. Both from and to are treated as descriptors co-occurring with their group; the result is a group × descriptor matrix. - Network graph — via create_network(edgelist), which treats from/to as directed node pairs and simplifies to an undirected graph.

Document this clearly in the vignette with a minimal worked example showing the rename step and both downstream calls.

A small anonymized example dataset can live in inst/extdata/ for use in tests and the vignette. Cluster IDs returned by run_pam_clustering() are integers; human-readable cluster labels are the caller’s responsibility and must not be hardcoded in the package.

What this package must NOT do

  • Import or depend on shiny, flexdashboard, or any other UI framework.
  • Produce flextable or officer output — table formatting belongs in the app.
  • Call set.seed() — reproducibility is the caller’s responsibility. Document this in the vignette.
  • Use print(), cat(), or message() for normal output — use rlang::inform() for optional diagnostics only, and keep them behind a verbose = FALSE argument.
  • Hardcode file paths or dataset-specific column names inside functions.

Versioning and changelog

Follow Semantic Versioning: MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH. Maintain a NEWS.md with a section per release in the format used by usethis::use_news_md().

Build sequence

Always build incrementally, phase by phase. Never write all files and run make check in one shot — errors become hard to untangle.

Each phase covers a coherent slice of the package. Complete each phase before starting the next: write or edit the relevant R/ file(s), then run the phase target to document and test that slice.

Phase make target R files Test filter
1 make phase1 utils-rank.R, tier.R tier
2 make phase2 ca_analysis.R, pam_clustering.R, anchoring.R anchoring
3 make phase3 network.R, integration.R integration
4 make phase4 leverage.R leverage
5 make phase5 palettes.R, vignettes/centr-pipeline.Rmd (vignette build)
Final make check all full R CMD check

Rules: - Run make document after every change to roxygen tags or exported function signatures, before running tests. - Run the phase-specific test target (e.g. make test-anchoring) while developing a single file. Run make test only when a phase is complete. - Do not proceed to the next phase until the current phase’s tests pass and make document produces no warnings. - Run make check only after all five phases are green. Resolve every NOTE, WARNING, and ERROR before committing. - The one acceptable NOTE is "New submission" on a first CRAN submission.

First-time setup:

make setup      # install declared dependencies
make document   # generate NAMESPACE and man/
make phase1     # verify the foundation before writing anything else

On this page

Developed by Jeremy Price.
